title: Reset, Revert, and Undoing Changes
content:
### git reset — Move the HEAD Pointer

`git reset` moves the current branch pointer backward, optionally modifying the staging area and working directory.

```bash
git reset --soft HEAD~1    # Move HEAD, keep changes staged
git reset --mixed HEAD~1   # Move HEAD, unstage changes (default)
git reset --hard HEAD~1    # Move HEAD, discard all changes
```

| Mode | HEAD | Staging | Working Dir |
|---|---|---|---|
| `--soft` | Moves | Kept | Kept |
| `--mixed` | Moves | Reset | Kept |
| `--hard` | Moves | Reset | Reset |

### git revert — Safe Undo

Revert creates a **new commit** that undoes the changes of a previous commit. Safe for public branches:

```bash
git revert HEAD                    # Undo last commit
git revert HEAD~3..HEAD            # Undo last 3 commits
git revert --no-commit <commit>    # Stage reversal without committing
```

### Reset vs Revert

| `git reset` | `git revert` |
|---|---|
| Rewrites history | Preserves history |
| Unsafe for public branches | Safe for public branches |
| Removes commits | Adds new undo commit |

### git restore

Modern alternative to checkout for restoring files:

```bash
git restore file.txt               # Discard unstaged changes
git restore --staged file.txt      # Unstage a file
git restore --source=HEAD~1 file   # Restore from a specific commit
```

### git clean

Remove untracked files from the working directory:

```bash
git clean -n                       # Dry run (show what would be removed)
git clean -f                       # Force remove
git clean -fd                      # Remove files and directories
```
codeExample:
# Undo last commit but keep changes
git reset --soft HEAD~1

# Undo last commit and discard changes
git reset --hard HEAD~1

# Safe undo for shared branches
git revert HEAD

# Unstage a file
git restore --staged file.txt

quiz.question: Which reset mode discards all changes in both staging and working directory?
Why is git revert preferred over git reset for public branches?
options: It preserves history with a new commit
It keeps the staging area clean
